steps: Preheat oven to ['temp']{175}. Grease a 9x5-inch loaf pan .

Brown the ['ing']{Unsalted butter}: Melt butter in saucepan over medium-low heat. Swirl pan occasionally until butter turns golden brown with nutty aroma (5-8 minutes). Transfer to heatproof bowl to cool.
In a large bowl, mash the ['ing']{Mashed overripe bananas} until smooth.
To the bananas, beat in the cooled brown butter, ['ing']{Sour cream}, ['ing']{Brown sugar}, ['ing']{Large egg}, and ['ing']{Vanilla extract}. Whisk until well combined.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the ['ing']{All-purpose flour}, ['ing']{Baking powder}, and ['ing']{Salt}.
Pour dry ingredients into wet ingredients and gently fold with a spatula until just combined. Do not overmix.
Gently fold in the ['ing']{Semi-sweet chocolate chips} until evenly distributed.
Pour batter into prepared pan and spread evenly. Top with additional chocolate chips.

Bake for 60-65 minutes, making sure to loosely cover the bread with aluminum foil halfway through to prevent the top from drying out. The bread is done when a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean with only a few small moist crumbs.
Cool in pan for 10 minutes, then transfer to wire rack to cool completely before slicing.
